name: Santillana Reservoir - Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Loop
name_original: Manzanares el Real, La Berzosa, Colmenar Viejo – ruta circular
description: This moderate 41.2-mile (66.3 km) road cycling route near Manzanares el Real offers scenic views and a good workout.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Manzanares El Real traverse diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park and the Cuenca Alta del río Manzanares Regional Park. The region features granite formations of La Pedriza, including hills and gorges, alongside the Santillana Lake. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from challenging mountain passes like Puerto de Navacerrada to more gentle routes around the reservoir. Best road cycling routes around Manzanares El Real The most popular road cycling route is Manzanares y Soto del Real, Miraflores de la Sierra, Rascafría, Cerceda – circular route, a 55.2 miles (88.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Santillana Reservoir - Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Loop, a moderate 41.2 miles (66.3 km) path. This route offers views of the Santillana Reservoir and the surrounding ash and oak forests. Local road cyclists also love the Manzanares el Real Castle – Alto del Cerro de San Pedro loop from Manzanares el Real, a 32.1 miles (51.6 km) trail leading through the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Manzanares El Real is defined by the granite formations of La Pedriza, the tranquil Santillana Lake, and challenging mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Manzanares El Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 100 reviews. More than 12,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Manzanares El Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Manzanares El Real?
A: There are over a dozen road cycling routes around Manzanares El Real, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 100 reviews.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Manzanares El Real?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from the granite formations of La Pedriza and the tranquil Santillana Lake to challenging mountain passes within the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. You'll find a mix of flat sections, rolling hills, and demanding climbs.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the area?
A: Yes, Manzanares El Real is known for its challenging climbs. Routes like the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977037630/manzanares-y-soto-del-real-miraflores-de-la-sierra-rascafria-cerceda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares y Soto del Real, Miraflores de la Sierra, Rascafría, Cerceda – circular route&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes. The area also provides access to mountain passes such as Puerto de Navacerrada, Puerto de Cotos, and Puerto de la Morcuera.

Q: Can beginners find suitable road cycling routes around Manzanares El Real?
A: Absolutely. While there are challenging routes, the area also offers easier options. The CiclaMadrid network, for instance, includes routes of lower difficulty that are perfect for exploring nearby villages and traditional trails. You can find several moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977468565/santillana-reservoir-cuenca-alta-del-manzanares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir - Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views without extreme climbs.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977037630/manzanares-y-soto-del-Real-miraflores-de-la-sierra-rascafria-cerceda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares y Soto del Real, Miraflores de la Sierra, Rascafría, Cerceda – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39565396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manzanares el Real Castle – Alto del Cerro de San Pedro loop from Manzanares el Real&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural features can I see while road cycling?
A: Road cycling routes often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can enjoy views of the unique granite formations of La Pedriza, including distinctive rock formations, and the serene Santillana Lake. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977468565/santillana-reservoir-cuenca-alta-del-manzanares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana Reservoir - Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers beautiful vistas of the reservoir and surrounding ash and oak forests.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can cycle past the impressive 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2714437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mendoza Castle&lt;/a&gt; in Manzanares El Real, one of the best-preserved castles in the Madrid region. Other historical sites include the Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de la Peña Sacra, offering views of La Pedriza, and the Church of Nuestra Señora de las Nieves.

Q: Can I find routes that connect Manzanares El Real to other towns?
A: Yes, the area features routes that connect Manzanares El Real to neighboring municipalities. For instance, you can find routes leading to towns like Torrelaguna or San Lorenzo de El Escorial, offering varied distances and elevation gains while showcasing scenic views and historical towns.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible near the road cycling routes?
A: While not directly on road cycling routes, the broader Manzanares El Real area is home to several beautiful waterfalls. Notable ones include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/851377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purgatory Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored as part of a combined cycling and hiking trip.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Manzanares El Real?
A: The diverse climate of the Sierra de Guadarrama means that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winters can bring colder temperatures and snow to higher mountain passes, requiring appropriate gear.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Manzanares El Real?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty, particularly the unique granite landscapes of La Pedriza, the varied terrain suitable for all skill levels, and the well-maintained routes that offer both challenging climbs and scenic, tranquil rides around places like the Santillana Reservoir.
tips: Probably its star monument due to its strange and unusual existence is the Entreterminos dolmen, an authentic megalith dating from around the 4th millennium BC. c.

The town is very charming with its stone houses and is full on weekends with people hiking, climbing and biking. In the square there is an Information Office where you can get a wide variety of information.
Factory bridge over the Manzanares on the M-618 between Colmenar Viejo and Hoyo del Manzanares. Wonderful views
A very nice reservoir. The sunsets and sunrises are spectacular, there are incredible views of Manzanares and its castle, and it is a good place to photograph birds.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate, requiring good fitness due to a significant elevation gain of approximately 680 meters over its 66.3 km length. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly on well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. You'll cycle through diverse landscapes including hills, gorges, steppes, pine forests, and bushes within the Sierra de Guadarrama region.

faqs.question: What are some notable sights along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting points, including the Collado Villalba Town Center, the historic Hoyo de Manzanares castle, and the Grajal Bridge. Keep an eye out for a View of the Lake and the Santillana Reservoir, though water levels can vary.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting Manzanares el Real, La Berzosa, and Colmenar Viejo, you can start and park in any of these towns. Manzanares el Real is a common starting point, offering various parking options. For public transport, check local bus services connecting to these towns.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring colder conditions, though the paved surfaces make it accessible year-round for prepared cyclists. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while dogs are generally not suitable companions for the full 66km ride, they are typically allowed in the towns and along public roads. If you plan to stop in natural areas, always keep your dog on a leash and follow local regulations for the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need special permits or to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily follows public roads and passes through the Parque Regional de la Cuenca Alta del Manzanares, which does not require entry fees for general access. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. Notably, it covers about 30% of the Ruta ADESGAM and also intersects with parts of the Ruta Verde 6.6 Carril Bici de Colmenar, Camino Mendocino, and the GR 124 MTB (Senda Real), among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your racebike, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A helmet is essential for safety.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects the towns of Manzanares el Real, La Berzosa, and Colmenar Viejo, you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in these settlements where you can stop for refreshments, food, or to refill your water bottles.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, significant length (66.3 km), and elevation gain (680 m), this route is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginner cyclists. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists with good fitness.
